data.title: American Express (NYSE: AXP) to report Q3 earnings on Oct. 17, expected at $4.00/share, revenue of $18.05B (up from $16.64B). Stock fell 2.3% to $323.12 on Thursday.
data.description: The American Express Company (NYSE: AXP) is set to announce its third-quarter earnings on October 17, before the market opens. Analysts predict earnings to be $4.00 per share, an increase from $3.49 per share a year ago. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be $18.05 billion, up from $16.64 billion in the previous year. American Express has surpassed revenue expectations for the last three consecutive quarters and in six of the last 10 quarters. On Thursday, the company's shares closed at $323.12, down by 2.3%. For further details and analyst ratings, visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Tomorrowland 2025 unites "People of Tomorrow" in Boom, Belgium, attracting hundreds of thousands to celebrate music and connection.
data.description: In Shenzhen, China, on October 17, 2025, it was reported that Tomorrowland, known for being more than just a music festival, serves as a global congregation where hundreds of thousands of "People of Tomorrow" convene in Boom, Belgium every summer to commemorate electronic music, creativity, and community. The festival draws in a significant number of attendees from around the world annually.

data.title: Dow falls over 300 points, Investor fear rises, Fear & Greed Index hits 'Extreme Fear' zone. Nov data shows -12.8 Philly Fed Manufacturing Index.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index experienced a rise in fear, entering the "Extreme Fear" zone on Thursday. U.S. stocks ended lower, with the Dow Jones index dropping over 300 points. Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE: SCHW) posted strong third-quarter earnings. The Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index dropped to -12.8 in October, hitting a six-month low compared to the previous month's 23.2 and falling short of the estimated 10. For a detailed report, visit Benzinga.com.
